name: Sherfield English
overview: Sherfield English is a small but well-served civil parish in the Test Valley district of Hampshire, lying along the historic Romsey-to-Salisbury road (the A27) about 5 miles north-west of Romsey, between the Test Valley and the New Forest. Largely rural and farming in character, with homes scattered along leafy lanes, it nonetheless offers a surprising array of amenities for its size and is known for a strong community spirit.
character: A charming, close-knit rural village with an unusually good range of amenities for its size, set in unspoilt Test Valley countryside.
amenities: Village shop with Post Office and farm shop
Tea rooms and a garden centre
Recreation ground/sports field and pavilion (well regarded)

schools.name: Awbridge Primary / Lockerley CofE Primary (nearest, both rated Good)
The Romsey School (secondary; Sherfield English is in catchment)

commutes.notes: Via Romsey, Winchester or Southampton Parkway stations

prices.summary: A desirable Test Valley village; period cottages, farmhouses and larger family homes along quiet lanes, with values supported by amenities and countryside. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Tea rooms and the garden centre
Walks between the Test Valley and New Forest
Easy trips to Romsey and Salisbury
placesToEat: Village tea rooms / Garden Room Cafe
Waitrose and eateries in nearby Romsey
pros: A surprising range of amenities for a small village (shop, pub, tea rooms, garden centre)
cons: No primary school within the village itself
No railway station; rely on Romsey/Winchester
Strung along the A27 with some through traffic
whoItSuits: Families and commuters wanting a friendly, well-equipped rural village between the Test Valley and New Forest with good road links.
primarySupermarket: Village shop and farm shop; Waitrose in Romsey
